How It Works

1

Browse Groups

View all 12 groups side-by-side. Each card shows the group letter, teams, and current standings.

2

Check Results

Played matches show full-time scores with goal details. Unplayed fixtures are marked TBD.

3

Track Standings

Tables update automatically with points, goals scored, goals conceded, and goal difference for every team.

Do's & Don'ts

вњ… Do's

  • Use the standings to plan your fantasy WC2026 team picks
  • Bookmark this page for quick access to live group tables
  • Share the page with friends to compare group predictions
  • Check back after each matchday for updated results

вќЊ Don'ts

  • Don't rely on unofficial sources вЂ” we verify every result
  • Don't refresh excessively; standings auto-update with new fixtures
  • Don't use outdated information вЂ” always check the badge for played matches

FAQ

When did the 2026 FIFA World Cup start?
The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicked off on June 11, 2026. It is the first edition to feature 48 teams across 12 groups of 4, hosted across Canada, Mexico, and the United States.
How are teams ranked in the group standings?
Teams are ranked by points (3 for a win, 1 for a draw, 0 for a loss), then goal difference (GD), then goals scored (GF). If still tied, head-to-head results, disciplinary points, and a drawing of lots may be used.
How many teams qualify from each group?
The top two teams from each of the 12 groups advance to the Round of 32. The 24 qualified teams are then joined by the 8 best third-placed teams to complete the knockout stage.
When will the remaining fixtures be played?
Group stage matches run from June 11 through July 2, 2026. Match schedules vary by group вЂ” check back as each group's fixtures are confirmed and added to the table.
Is this page updated in real time?
Yes. Our team monitors official FIFA sources and updates results, standings, and goal data promptly after each match concludes. The "matches played" badge shows how many group fixtures have been completed.
Where can I find old World Cup group standings?
This page covers the 2026 edition only. For historical results, visit FIFA's official archives or check our related football tools section below.
